# Phase 10 Runtime Eligibility Plan

Phase: 10.0.37 - Constitutional Runtime Eligibility Review, Activation Boundary & Execution Readiness Doctrine  
Status: Planning-only. Runtime remains disabled.

## Objective

Define future runtime eligibility conditions while preserving the separation between eligibility, authorization, implementation, activation, and execution.

## Scope

- Runtime eligibility doctrine.
- Activation boundary philosophy.
- Execution readiness standards.
- Core vs industry module boundaries.
- Shared core protection.
- Industry module non-mutation.
- Adapter eligibility.
- Runtime preconditions.
- Approval-gated activation.
- Provider readiness gates.
- Human review before execution.
- Fail-closed eligibility.
- Non-eligibility conditions.
- Constitutional execution boundary.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs, providers, adapters, credentials, or OAuth.
- Prisma, Supabase, Twilio, email, or background jobs.
- Autonomous runtime eligibility engines.
- Automatic readiness approval.
- Execution authority.
- Deployment authority.
- Provider onboarding.
- Industry-module execution.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Planning docs remain isolated under governance documentation paths.
- Eligibility remains separate from authorization.
- Shared core infrastructure remains protected.
- Industry modules cannot mutate the shared core.
- Adapter and provider boundaries remain reviewable and non-executing.
- Runtime remains disabled.
